Purpose Statement: This organization will provide a platform for students to collaborate on software projects independent of the OSU curriculum for the purpose of experience and portfolio-building. Meetings will be held every week; members will present their progress on current projects before splitting up into project groups. While some coding experience is recommended, we will accept members of all skill levels.

Meeting Information.Meeting Time and Place: Wednesday, 6:00-7:30pm, Friday 6:00pm-7:30pm, 2009 Millikin Road, Columbus, OH 43210

Membership Details.Time of Year for New Membership: Students can become members at any time of the year.
Membership Details.How does a Prospective Member Apply: No applications needed! Join our discord at https://discord.gg/ZU8fCvceQJ

Purpose Statement: Buckeye FinTech is dedicated to fostering a community of students who are interested in exploring the field of financial technology. Our mission is to empower members by providing them with practical skills and knowledge through interactive workshops, networking events, and collaborative projects. We aim to bridge the fields of finance and technology in order to contribute meaningful insights to the FinTech ecosystem.

Our purpose is to facilitate discussion, learning, and interest in the field of artificial intelligence, organize AI experts and researchers to present their findings and thoughts to educate students, to build and assist others with projects related to artificial intelligence, and to connect students with opportunities to do research in this field. Some examples of the events we host include speakers within the field, artificial intelligence technology workshops to teach students how to use and incorporate AI, and more.
Office Location: 395 Dreese Laboratory, 2015 Neil Avenue, Columbus, OH 43210

Membership Details.Time of Year for New Membership: New members are welcome at every meeting. Come to meetings to learn more about the perks of being an official Active Member!
Membership Details.How does a Prospective Member Apply: No application/selection process. Just come to our weekly meetings and events! WWednesday 7-8pm @ Baker Systems 198 or Zoom. Registration Form: https://go.osu.edu/aiclubnew Slack: https://go.osu.edu/aiclubslack Newsletter: https://go.osu.edu/aiclub

Club Name: The AutoDrive Challenge Team at The Ohio State University
Purpose Statement: Ever wonder how an Autonomous Vehicle works? How engineers ensure that the vehicle makes the right decisions? Want to get exposure into this new and exciting space? The Buckeye AutoDrive team at The Ohio State University is one of ten collegiate motorsport teams in North America taking part in the SAE/GM AutoDrive Challenge II. Over the course of 5 years, we are tasked with converting a 2022 Chevy Bolt EUV into a fully autonomous (Level 4) vehicle. We bring students from various disciplines (Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and other majors) to achieve this feat.

Meeting Information.Meeting Time and Place: Sundays 1:30 to 4:30pm, 930 Kinnear Road, Columbus, OH 43210

Membership Details.Membership Contact: If you are interested in joining the team, please contact Gowrav Mannem (Lead Project Manager) at mannem.3@osu.edu or Shreya Rawale (Sr. Project Manager) at rawale.5@osu.edu.
Membership Details.Time of Year for New Membership: We are open to onboard new team members throughout the academic year. However, we primarily focus on onboarding during the first 3 weeks of the Autumn Semester.
Membership Details.How does a Prospective Member Apply: New team members will go through 2 onboarding sessions to learn how the team and vehicle functions. After this, students will be asked to pick open positions on the team that match their interests. Team Leaders will try their best to place members into the role they are interested while ensuring the entire team is staffed appropriately. Students will then undergo 4-6 weeks of training, After this, they are officially a member of the team.

Purpose Statement: We aim to inspire students to think analytically, empower them through hands on training, and connect them to potential employers.

Meeting Information.Meeting Time and Place: Tuesdays at 7:30pm, 1760 Neil Ave Columbus, OH 43210, Columbus, OH 43210

Office Location: 1760 Neil Ave, Columbus, OH 43210, Columbus, OH 43210

Membership Details.Time of Year for New Membership: Meetings are always open to new members
Membership Details.How does a Prospective Member Apply: Our meetings are open to everyone

Purpose Statement: The Sport Club program is designed to serve individual interests in different sports and recreational activities. Participation in the program enhances and promotes the element of good sportsmanship, the development of skills, the well-being derived from physical activity, and the social aspect within each sport. If competition is desired, opportunities exist in varying degrees within individual clubs.

Meeting Information.Meeting Time and Place: W 7:30 PM-10:00 PM Su 10:00 AM-12:00 PM, 855 Woody Hayes Drive, Columbus, OH 43210, Columbus, OH 43210

Membership Details.Time of Year for New Membership: Must attend tryouts at the beginning of the fall semester or request private tryout.
Membership Details.How does a Prospective Member Apply: Tryouts will be held in the Fall shortly following the start of school.
